info.name: RTP Ledger
info.description:
Tests the rtp-client Spring Boot module: POST ledger posting (Disruptor → NATS) and GET balance (NATS request to server).

**Prerequisites:** Client running on `baseUrl` (Docker Compose default http://localhost:18080; local `mvn spring-boot:run` default http://localhost:8080). For GET balance to return 200, the CP-03 server must be running and subscribed to `ledger.balance.>`. NATS must be reachable at `rtp.client.nats.servers` in application.yml.

**Path `accountId`:** Publish subject is `ledger.{region}.{accountId}`; use the same id as in your test scenario (often the credited account UUID).

variable.description: Seeded account (ACC-ca-east-00) used as POST path account and live balance lookup account
Seeded credited account (ACC-ca-east-00). Keep aligned with accountId for demo consistency.

request.description:
Passes `BianTransactionValidator` (8 rules). Response **202** with `status: ACCEPTED` when the Disruptor accepts the event. If the ring buffer is full, response is **503** with `status: OVERLOADED`.

Duplicate runs: change `messageId`, `paymentInformationId`, or `endToEndId` if you need unique business keys.

Intentionally violates multiple rules (blank endToEndId, non-positive amount, invalid currency, self-transfer, blank paymentInformationId, null localInstrument). Expect **400** with `status: REJECTED` and an `errors` array.

Synchronous NATS request to subject `ledger.balance.{region}.{accountId}` (500 ms timeout). Without CP-03 server + NATS, expect **504** Gateway Timeout or **502** from the client. With server replying JSON matching `BalanceResponse`, expect **200**.
Client service health endpoint. Expect 200 with `{"status":"UP"}` when the client is running.
Starts the Apple Pay burst simulation (500 micro-transactions in 2s, 3s lull, repeated 3 times).
Starts the 60-second mixed scenario (200 virtual users across 10 accounts).
Starts 1000 sequential transactions on one account and verifies final balance.
Returns active scenario, TPS, elapsed time, and success/failure counters.
Subscribes to live simulator events using SSE. Keep request open to watch updates.
Simulator service health endpoint. Expect 200 with `{"status":"UP"}` when simulator is running.
